summary: Initial MVP testing for Esports Bet platform. Identified CRITICAL backend bug: get_current_user() sets user['_id'] but all downstream auth-protected endpoints reference user['id'], causing 500 errors on /auth/me, POST /games, all /tournaments endpoints, /chat, /wallet/deposit, /wallet/transactions. Public endpoints (register, login, list games, list tournaments, leaderboard) and login brute-force work correctly. Frontend landing/register pages load fine.

critical.issue: KeyError: 'id'. get_current_user() (server.py L65-70) does user['_id'] = str(user['_id']) but endpoints use user['id']. Causes 500 across /auth/me, POST /games, POST/GET-detail/join/complete /tournaments, POST /chat, POST /wallet/deposit, GET /wallet/transactions. Fix: add user['id'] = str(user['_id']) before returning, or rename consistently.

minor.issue: winner_user_id is a query param (not body) - inconsistent API design; should be Pydantic model
Allows stake_amount=0 which bypasses escrow logic - should validate amount > 0
No validation that winner_user_id is actually a participant in the tournament
No check that sender is a participant of the tournament - any authenticated user can post to any tournament chat
secure=False on production HTTPS - cookies should be Secure=True (and samesite='none' for cross-site if needed)
Reset link only printed to console (no email integration) - acceptable for MVP but flag for prod

ui_bugs.issue: Missing data-testid attributes on inputs and submit buttons - hampers automated testing

integration_issues.flow: Any authenticated flow (Dashboard, Wallet, BrowseGames, CreateTournament, etc.)
integration_issues.issue: All blocked by backend critical bug above - /auth/me returns 500, so authenticated UI cannot bootstrap user state

action_items: URGENT: Fix get_current_user() in /app/backend/server.py to include user['id'] = str(user['_id']) (or refactor consistently). This single fix likely unblocks ~90% of failing endpoints.
Add data-testid attributes to login/register/dashboard interactive elements
Validate winner_user_id is a participant in /tournaments/{id}/complete
Restrict chat posting to tournament participants only
Change complete-tournament winner_user_id from query param to request body
Set Secure=True on cookies for HTTPS production environment
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~700 lines mixing auth/games/tournaments/chat/wallet - split into routers/modules
Repeated ObjectId(user['id']) + user lookups in tournaments/chat - extract helpers
No async error handling for Stripe API failures - bare 500s leak internals
Tournament creator auto-deducted stake but on failure to insert participants, partial state remains (no transaction)
No rate limiting on /wallet/deposit - users could spam Stripe sessions
FRONTEND_URL has fallback default 'http://localhost:3000' in CORS - protected vars should fail fast

success_rate.frontend: not fully tested - blocked by backend auth bug

test_credentials: admin@esportsbet.com / admin123 (login works, but /auth/me fails after due to critical bug)
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed users and a TEST_FIFA24 game attempt (failed due to bug)
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes get_current_user user['id'] bug, re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py - most failures should resolve. Then proceed with frontend E2E: register -> dashboard -> create game -> create tournament -> join -> chat -> complete -> wallet deposit flow. Stripe deposit can be partially tested up to checkout URL generation; actual payment polling requires real Stripe interaction.
rca of the issue: RCA: In server.py line 65-70, get_current_user() loads user from MongoDB then mutates _id field (user['_id'] = str(user['_id'])) but never sets user['id']. However, all 9 protected endpoints reference user['id']. Reproduction: register a user (sets cookie) -> GET /api/auth/me -> 500 KeyError: 'id'. Mitigation: add `user['id'] = str(user['_id'])` after line 68 in get_current_user(), keeping _id stringified for backward compat or removing it.
